title: jatropha palustris
intro: Jatropha palustris, also known as the marsh stinking rose or poison nut, is a plant species belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Jatropha palustris include the potential treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic and insect repellent. Herbal preparations of Jatropha palustris can be made into a poultice, tincture, or infusion, and are typically used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
